notes: v1.13.7 — FULL-BLEED LANDING (kill the bottom dead-band). #boot globe-landing only, surgical: .pl-stage now flex-fills the boot column (flex:1 1 auto; min-height:0) instead of the capped clamp(380px,55vh,540px) that left an empty band below on tall / desktop viewports (also dodges the iOS vh-behind-toolbar bug). Globe radius made clip-safe: R = min(W*0.45, H*0.40) so the halo ring is never crowded or cut when the stage grows tall. #boot left untouched (position:fixed inset:0 + justify-content:flex-start): header pins top, stage fills the rest, stats at stage-top, hint near the bottom. Behavior unchanged: spin-to-online -> lock -> warp, the UFO + ambient space layer, all inputs (drag / scroll / keys, no-WebGL stage-tap), prefers-reduced-motion. Version skipped v1.13.6 per the task. Verified: node --check 3/3 inline scripts, CSS braces balanced, CRLF preserved on dct-ios.html and sw.js (0 lone LF; version.json stays LF as it always was), stamps lockstep at v1.13.7, sw.js cache-bumped. NOT device-verified -- WebGL is not renderable here; needs an on-device pass on iPhone Safari (portrait), desktop, and a range of window heights. Residual: #boot keeps its safe-area + 24px bottom padding (per spec, #boot untouched), so the stage fills to that inset, not literally to the glass edge.
